title: Windows Security, Permissions, Encryption, and Recovery · Support Workbench
summary: A guided support bench extends Windows endpoint security and recovery baseline while retaining authorization, safety, baseline, privacy, accessibility, rollback, verification, documentation, and user communication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of Windows endpoint security and recovery baseline while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure standard and administrative accounts, UAC, Windows Hello, MFA-capable identity, lock, session, sign-in options, recovery, and accessible alternatives.
Reason about users, groups, ownership, inheritance, explicit allow and deny, effective NTFS and share access, elevation, and least privilege.
Plan BitLocker scope, TPM use, startup protection, recovery-key custody, escrow, suspension, hardware change, backup, verification, and disposition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Account Security
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Sensitive Windows data has inherited share permissions, a missing recovery key, weakened protection, and an inaccessible sign-in fallback. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Account Security.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for configure standard and administrative accounts, uac, windows hello, mfa-capable identity, lock, session, sign-in options, recovery, and accessible alternatives. For CompTIA A+ support Commit to the mission model · Workshop Windows Account Security, keep Windows Account Security connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Sensitive Windows data has inherited share permissions, a missing recovery key, weakened protection, and an inaccessible sign-in fallback. The workshop variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: defend one safe causal support decision
